rationale: The one substantive ground-truth concern (dsocek's point that the hardcoded default_components list duplicates logic and should instead be driven from a canonical per-model source, e.g. so naming variants like unet are handled generically) is captured in spirit by Candidate X's comment #1 (reuse pipeline._dit_modules instead of a fourth hardcoded list), which X grounds with specific file/line evidence. Candidate Y never raises this architectural concern at all, instead offering test-coverage (round-trip activation test) and DRY (parametrize) nits that don't appear in the ground truth. Both candidates' factual claims (e.g. pipeline_sdxl.py:110) are mutually consistent and plausible, so precision is comparable and reasonably high for both, but X's actionability edges out due to the higher-value architectural fix suggestion versus Y's lower-impact nits and verbose non-actionable 'validated' bullet list.
